yesterdayAnalysis: Rest day following Oregon 70.3 on Sunday. Zero training is exactly what Week 1 recovery demands — you raced a PR 70.3 two days ago and your body needs complete rest right now. Sleep score rebounded to 76 from race night's 49, which is a good sign recovery is beginning.
todayRecommendation: Today's plan calls for ROUVY bike + brick run + 3,000m pool swim. That is way too much for Day 3 post-race recovery. Skip the brick entirely and do easy pool swimming only if you feel genuinely recovered — 2,000-2,500m aerobic, no intervals. Your sleep is still normalizing and you should not be forcing volume this week.
weekProgress: Week 1 of 13: 0 TSS of 150 target with 4 days remaining including today. This is mandatory post-race recovery — zero volume through Tuesday is 100% correct and exactly as planned. Do not chase the 150 TSS target this week if your body is telling you to rest more. Recovery trumps arbitrary numbers right now.
keyInsight: You are 3 days post-PR at Oregon 70.3 — your only job this week is to recover fully, not hit TSS targets or maintain fitness.
alerts: Sleep score averaged 64/100 over 7 days with race week disruption (scores of 34, 48, 49) — continue prioritizing sleep this week
SpO2 dipped to 90% on July 18 and 92% on race night — monitor for pattern but likely race stress related
